frameworks.name: dual-stream (ventral/dorsal) architecture
frameworks.one_line: two parallel pathways encode object identity and relational/numerical structure separately

frameworks.name: deep neural network modeling of primate vision
frameworks.one_line: extends object-recognition CNN modeling to numerosity and scene structure

frameworks.name: neural number coding
frameworks.one_line: lognormal tuning to numerosity in posterior parietal cortex

core_question: Can a dual-stream deep network count items zero-shot and reproduce primate parietal number codes?
key_assumptions: object recognition and relational/numerical encoding are dissociable across streams
scene understanding requires both item identity and abstract numerical structure
deep networks can serve as models of primate visual cortex
key_findings: dual-stream model counts unfamiliar objects zero-shot
model develops spatial response fields resembling parietal cortex
model produces lognormal number codes like macaque posterior parietal cortex
interpretations: dual processing streams jointly support object recognition and abstract numerical structure encoding
zero-shot counting can emerge from biologically inspired architecture without item-specific training
